chapter_title.en: Chapter on Establishing Ihram, Its Conditions, Nullification, and the Prayer for it

text: 2565 - وَ رَوَى حَفْصُ بْنُ اَلْبَخْتَرِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِيمَنْ عَقَدَ اَلْإِحْرَامَ فِي مَسْجِدِ اَلشَّجَرَةِ ثُمَّ وَقَعَ عَلَى أَهْلِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُلَبِّيَ قَالَ «لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2565 - Hafs ibn al-Bakhtari narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding someone who entered Ihram at Masjid al-Shajarah and then had intercourse with his spouse before reciting the Talbiyah.
Imam (as) said: "There is nothing upon him [no penalty]."
